title: Plakar PostgreSQL AWS IAM Importer Connector Config

location.description: PostgreSQL connection URI: postgres+aws://[user@]host[:port][/database]. No password should be provided — the IAM authentication token is fetched automatically from AWS.

host.description: RDS instance hostname (default: localhost). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: RDS instance port (default: 5432). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: PostgreSQL username to authenticate as (required). Must be an IAM-enabled database user. Overrides the username in the location URI.

region.description: AWS region of the RDS instance (required), e.g. us-east-1. Passed to `aws rds generate-db-auth-token --region`.

database.description: Name of the database to back up using pg_dump. If omitted, all databases are backed up using pg_dumpall. Overrides the database in the location URI.

exclude_databases.description: Comma-separated list of database names to skip during a full backup. Defaults to 'rdsadmin' (an internal AWS system database that cannot be dumped by regular users). Set to an empty string to disable all exclusions.

schema_only.description: When true, passes -s to pg_dump/pg_dumpall to dump only schema (no data). Mutually exclusive with data_only.

data_only.description: When true, passes -a to pg_dump/pg_dumpall to dump only data (no schema). Mutually exclusive with schema_only.

pg_bin_dir.description: Directory containing the PostgreSQL client binaries (pg_dump, pg_dumpall, psql). When omitted, binaries are resolved via $PATH.

compress.description: Enable pg_dump compression (default: false). By default dumps are uncompressed so that Plakar's own compression is not degraded.

ssl_mode.description: SSL mode for the connection, passed via PGSSLMODE. IAM authentication requires an encrypted connection — use `require` or higher.

ssl_cert.description: Path to the client SSL certificate file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLCERT.

ssl_key.description: Path to the client SSL private key file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLKEY.

ssl_root_cert.description: Path to the root CA certificate used to verify the server (PEM format), passed via PGSSLROOTCERT.
